---
title: "1.12 Objects: Instances of Classes"
subtitle: "Part 1: Classes, Objects, Attributes, and Behaviors"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit1-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### Java is object-oriented

Java is an **object-oriented programming** language.

That means that one of the primary ways of designing and organizing a Java program is in terms of **objects** (对象).

Objects combine data and the code that operates on that data into a single unit.

To create objects, we first define a **class** (类), which provides a blueprint for creating the objects.

## Programs Built from Classes

### `public class`

In Java, all programs are built out of classes.

This is why every Java program starts with:

```java
public class
```

In this unit, you will learn the vocabulary of object-oriented programming and how to create and use objects of a class written for you.

In later units, you will learn to write your own classes.

## Classes and Objects

### Blueprint for creating objects

You can think of a class as something like a blueprint of a house that is used to construct houses, which are objects.

![Blueprint class model](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-12-objects-instances-of-classes/blueprint.png){fig-align="center" width="46%"}

The class gives the plan; the objects are specific instances created from that plan.

## Classes and Objects

### Cookie cutter model

You can also think of a class like a cookie cutter that is used to create cookies, which are objects.

![Cookie cutter class model](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-12-objects-instances-of-classes/cookieCutterLabelled.png){fig-align="center" width="58%"}

The cookie cutter, the class, can be used to create as many cookies, the objects, as you want.

The objects are all the same shape and size, but they can have different colors and decorations.

## Class as a Data Type

### `Turtle` variables

You can also think of a class as defining a new data type.

In this lesson, we will use the class `Turtle` to make animated turtle objects.

Just like you use `int` to declare variables that hold numbers, you can use `Turtle` to declare variables that can refer to animated turtle objects.

```java
// Creating 2 Turtle objects called yertle and myrtle
Turtle yertle;
Turtle myrtle;
```

These animated turtle objects are **instances** (实例) of the `Turtle` class.

## Attributes and Behaviors

### Class definition vocabulary

A class defines the **attributes** (data) and **behaviors** (methods) that all objects of that class will have.

The objects are the specific **instances** of the class that have their own values for the attributes.

**Attributes** are the data or properties that an object knows about itself.

**Behaviors** are the things that an object can do.

## Attributes and Behaviors

### Turtle examples

For a turtle object:

::: {.tight-list}
- attributes include color and size
- behaviors include going forward, turning left, and turning right
:::

The attributes and behaviors of a class are defined in the class, but each object has its own values for the attributes.

## Cat Objects

### Shared attributes and behaviors

The following picture has lots of cats, which are objects of the type cat.

They are all different, but they share the same attributes and behaviors that make up a cat.

![Pictures of cat objects](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-12-objects-instances-of-classes/catsLabelled.png){fig-align="center" width="64%"}

They are all instances of cat with different values for their attributes.

## Student Response

### `shortanswer:: cats`

Discuss with your class:

::: {.tight-list}
- What are some attributes of cats?
- What are some behaviors of cats?
- For one visible cat, name an attribute and its attribute value.
:::

Attributes are often nouns or adjectives describing features.

Behaviors are often verbs.

## Turtle Object Model

### Attributes and methods

The following picture of a turtle shows some Turtle **attributes** like `name`, `width`, `height`, and color in the body of the turtle.

It also shows **methods** like `forward()` and `backward()` around the turtle.

![Turtle attributes and behaviors](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-12-objects-instances-of-classes/turtleOOD.png){fig-align="center" width="46%"}

## Vocabulary Check

### `dragndrop:: vocab2_1`

Match each definition to the correct concept.

::: {.table-fit}
| Definition | Concept |
|---|---|
| a specific instance of a class with defined attributes | object |
| defines a new data type that is like a blueprint | class |
| define what the object knows about itself | attributes or instance variables |
| define what an object can do | behaviors or methods |
:::

## Quick Check

### `mchoice:: howmanyobjects`

How many objects can you create from a class in Java?

::: {.tight-list}
- A. `1`
- B. `10`
- C. `1000`
- D. As many as you need
:::

Correct answer: D. You can create as many objects as you need from one class.

## Quick Check

### `mchoice:: mcq_behaviors`

What specifies the behavior for objects of a class in Java?

::: {.tight-list}
- A. attributes
- B. methods
- C. class
- D. object
:::

Correct answer: B. Methods specify the behavior of all objects of a class.

## Quick Check

### `mchoice:: mcq_data`

What are the data or properties of an object called?

::: {.tight-list}
- A. attributes
- B. methods
- C. class
- D. object
:::

Correct answer: A. Attributes specify the data that an object keeps track of.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- define a class as a blueprint for objects
- define an object as a specific instance of a class
- explain that classes can define new data types
- distinguish attributes from behaviors
- connect attributes with data and instance variables
- connect behaviors with methods
:::

## End

### 1.12 Part 1 complete

Part 2 continues with the `Turtle` class and object method calls.
